overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete (Section 9(B)) may not be enforceable under California law.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may freely compete post-termination despite the 2-year noncompete in the FA.

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses (Section 6(I)(1)(d) and 8(B)(3)) may be unenforceable under California Civil Code §1671.
overrides.why_it_matters: The aggressive 36-month liquidated damages formula may be void in CA.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration venue in Aberdeen, SD may not be enforceable under CA law. Governing law (SD) may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to arbitrate/litigate in California under California law.

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp. Code §31512) and Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code §20010).
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer release cannot waive CA franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the Franchise Agreement.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides SD governing law provision for IL franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Venue outside Illinois is void. However, arbitration may take place outside IL.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be required to litigate in SD. Arbitration in SD remains possible.

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: any provision binding franchisee to waive compliance with IL Franchise Disclosure Act is void.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law protections cannot be waived.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year statute of limitations.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees have MD forum available for franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: General release as condition of renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims survive renewal/transfer releases.

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause. Good cause includes failure to comply with lawful provision with 30-day cure notice.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI provides stronger termination protection than the base FA.

overrides.override_summary: Non-renewal requires fair market value compensation for inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures (if term < 5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under different brand, or < 6 months notice).
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees may receive compensation upon non-renewal.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be required to resolve disputes in SD.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee cannot consent to liquidated damages. Only damages allowed under MN law. Cannot consent to injunctions.
overrides.why_it_matters: The 36-month liquidated damages formula may be unenforceable for MN franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be required outside Minnesota. MN law and procedures preserved.
overrides.why_it_matters: SD venue/law provisions overridden for MN franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release/assignment/novation/waiver relieving any person from MN statutory liability.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law protections survive releases.

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice with 60-day cure. 180-day non-renewal notice. Transfer consent not unreasonably withheld.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN provides longer notice periods than the base FA's 30-day cure.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY adds a franchisee termination right not in the base FA.

overrides.override_summary: General Business Law Article 33 rights (§687.4, §687.5) preserved despite renewal/transfer releases.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law protections cannot be waived via release.

overrides.override_summary: Covenants not to compete contrary to NDCC 9-08-06 or unfair/unjust/inequitable are generally unenforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees likely cannot be bound by the 2-year post-term noncompete.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreeable site (not remote from franchisee). Non-arbitration litigation in ND.
overrides.why_it_matters: SD venue overridden — disputes resolved in or near ND.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law governs for ND franchises.
overrides.why_it_matters: SD governing law overridden.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require consent to termination or liquidated damages.
overrides.why_it_matters: 36-month liquidated damages formula may be unenforceable in ND.

overrides.override_summary: Venue outside RI void for claims enforceable under RI Franchise Investment Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can bring claims in RI courts.

overrides.override_summary: Application of laws of another state void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: SD law does not apply to RI franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for reasonable cause per Article 13.1-564 Virginia Retail Franchising Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination grounds must constitute 'reasonable cause' under VA law.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation in WA or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: SD venue overridden for WA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver void except in negotiated settlements with independent counsel after agreement is in effect.
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer releases are largely void in WA.

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void unless employee earns $100K+/year (employee) or $250K+/year (independent contractor). Adjusted for inflation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete likely unenforceable for most WA hotel employees.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate for any grounds under state law. Section 8(C) added: franchisee may terminate for material franchisor default with 90-day cure.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees have an explicit termination right for franchisor breach — the only state addendum adding this.

overrides.override_summary: Treble damages available under RCW 19.100.190. Waivers of exemplary/punitive damages void.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can seek enhanced damages.
